message:
test: enhance E2E test infrastructure and close remaining beads

- Updated bead tracker with closed issues (bd-d1p0, bd-397z, bd-2ei4, bd-25m4, bd-1ci7)
- Integrate terminal raw mode and PTY test improvements from parallel agents

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -58,12 +58,12 @@
 {"id":"bd-1ok","title":"demo_showcase spec: visual design system (palette, typography, spacing, components)","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — Visual Design System

## Goal

A consistent, modern visual language that makes OpenTUI feel premium:

- strong spacing rhythm
- crisp borders
- tasteful gradients
- glassy overlays
- clear focus/selection

This spec defines **multiple themes** because the demo explicitly showcases theme switching (UI + highlighting) as a real app capability.

## Theme Set

### Theme A: “Synthwave Professional” (Default, Dark)

Base background:
- BG0 = #0f1220 (app background)
- BG1 = #151a2e (panel background)
- BG2 = #1d2440 (raised surfaces / cards)

Text:
- FG0 = #e6e6e6 (primary)
- FG1 = #aeb6d6 (secondary)
- FG2 = #6c7396 (dim)

Accents:
- Aqua   = #4dd6ff
- Pink   = #ff4fd8
- Green  = #2bff88
- Orange = #ffb020
- Red    = #ff4455

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #2a335c (typically applied as a low-alpha overlay)
- focus border: Aqua (and bold)

### Theme B: “Light (Paper)”

Base background:
- BG0 = #f7f7fb
- BG1 = #ffffff
- BG2 = #eef0f7

Text:
- FG0 = #1a1b26
- FG1 = #3a3f5a
- FG2 = #6a6f8a

Accents:
- Blue   = #2a6fff
- Purple = #7b61ff
- Green  = #00a86b
- Orange = #ff8a00
- Red    = #e53935

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #dbe6ff (low alpha)
- focus border: Blue

### Theme C: “Solarized-ish” (Low Eye Strain)

Base background:
- BG0 = #002b36
- BG1 = #073642
- BG2 = #0b4452

Text:
- FG0 = #eee8d5
- FG1 = #93a1a1
- FG2 = #657b83

Accents:
- Cyan   = #2aa198
- Yellow = #b58900
- Orange = #cb4b16
- Red    = #dc322f
- Blue   = #268bd2

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #0d5161 (low alpha)
- focus border: Cyan

### Theme D: “High Contrast” (Accessibility / Worst-Case Terminals)

Base background:
- BG0 = #000000
- BG1 = #000000
- BG2 = #111111

Text:
- FG0 = #ffffff
- FG1 = #e0e0e0
- FG2 = #a0a0a0

Accents:
- Cyan   = #00ffff
- Yellow = #ffff00
- Magenta= #ff00ff
- Green  = #00ff00
- Red    = #ff0000

Selection + Focus:
- selection bg: #333333
- focus border: Yellow

## Component Styling Tokens (Theme-Relative)

- Panel border: dim FG2
- Focused panel border: focus accent + bold + optional 1px “glow line”
- Headers: gradient bar + bold title
- Status bar: BG2 with muted text; hotkeys in FG0 bold
- Toasts: rounded box + opacity stack (glass)

## Typography / Characters

- Prefer Unicode box drawing (rounded/heavy where appropriate).
- Use subtle separators: │, ─, ·.
- Keep iconography mostly to single-codepoint symbols for stable width.

## Gradient Rules

- Gradients should be subtle: 5–10% perceived change across width.
- Use Rgba::lerp between two close hues.

## Theme Switching Rules

- Theme switching changes BOTH:
  1) UI palette/token set
  2) syntax highlighting theme (Theme::dark/light/…)

The demo should present theme switching as a real workflow feature:

- via command palette
- optionally via number keys when help is open (discoverable)

## Degradation Rules (Terminal Capability Variance)

- If no truecolor, map theme colors to the closest 256-color approximation.
- Avoid relying on italics/strikethrough; treat as “bonus” if supported.

## What This Bead Produces

- Canonical theme token sets used everywhere in the demo.
- A single “current theme” selection mechanism shared by chrome, panels, overlays, toasts, and highlighting.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Palette tokens defined (BG/FG/Accents)
- [ ] Focus/selection styling rules defined
- [ ] Component styling rules defined (borders, headers, status, toasts)
- [ ] Gradient rules defined
- [ ] Degradation rules defined","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.420443553Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:18:02.358210255Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:18:02.358144072Z","close_reason":"Completed: Created docs/DEMO_SHOWCASE_DESIGN_SYSTEM.md with 4 theme definitions, component styling, typography, spacing, gradients, and degradation rules","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ok","depends_on_id":"bd-2jc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:06:10.450428345Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ok","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:58.107964883Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1pd","title":"demo_showcase UI: implement design system (palette + style helpers + gradients)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Implement Design System

## Purpose

Turn the design system spec (`bd-1ok`) into code inside the demo binary:

- theme token sets (multiple themes)
- color tokens (`Rgba` constants)
- style builders (header style, border style, focus style, selection style)
- gradient helpers using `Rgba::lerp`

## Implementation Pattern

Keep it self-contained inside `src/bin/demo_showcase.rs`:

- `enum UiTheme { SynthwaveDark, PaperLight, Solarized, HighContrast }`
- `struct Tokens { bg0, bg1, bg2, fg0, fg1, fg2, accent_primary, ... }`
- `impl UiTheme { fn tokens(self, caps: &Capabilities) -> Tokens }`

No file proliferation unless truly necessary.

## Highlight Theme Mapping

Theme switching should update BOTH:

1) UI palette tokens
2) syntax highlighting theme used by the editor panel

Current highlight themes available in the library are:

- Theme::dark()
- Theme::light()
- Theme::high_contrast()

So the mapping should be:

- UiTheme::SynthwaveDark -> Theme::dark()
- UiTheme::PaperLight -> Theme::light()
- UiTheme::Solarized -> Theme::dark() (closest available unless we add a Solarized highlight theme later)
- UiTheme::HighContrast -> Theme::high_contrast()

## Must Demonstrate

- Truecolor gradients when available (still okay if approximated)
- cohesive spacing and hierarchy
- strong focus/selection affordances that remain readable in reduced-color terminals

Dependencies:
- This bead is blocked on the design system spec.","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Palette constants exist and match spec
- [ ] Style helpers exist (borders, focus, headers, status, toasts)
- [ ] Gradient helper(s) exist and are used in top bar or background","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"RusticSnow","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:42.059622696Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:06.238177379Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T01:13:06.238002503Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1pd","depends_on_id":"bd-1ok","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:31:49.547954895Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1pd","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:42.071894164Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1qe","title":"Add Terminal Cursor Save/Restore Methods","description":"## Overview
Add Terminal methods for cursor save/restore. The ANSI sequences already exist.

## Already Implemented (NO WORK NEEDED)
In `src/ansi/sequences.rs`:
- `CURSOR_SAVE: &str = "\x1b7"` ✓
- `CURSOR_RESTORE: &str = "\x1b8"` ✓

## Scope (Methods to Add)
```rust
// In src/terminal/mod.rs
impl Terminal<W> {
    /// Save cursor position using DEC sequence.
    pub fn save_cursor(&mut self) -> io::Result<()> {
        self.write_all(CURSOR_SAVE.as_bytes())
    }

    /// Restore cursor position using DEC sequence.
    pub fn restore_cursor(&mut self) -> io::Result<()> {
        self.write_all(CURSOR_RESTORE.as_bytes())
    }
}
```

## Files to Modify
- src/terminal/mod.rs - Add the 2 methods

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_save_cursor_sequence() {
    let mut output = Vec::new();
    let mut terminal = Terminal::new(&mut output);
    
    terminal.save_cursor().unwrap();
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Output bytes: {:?}", output);
    assert_eq!(output, b"\x1b7");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: save_cursor writes correct sequence");
}

#[test]
fn test_restore_cursor_sequence() {
    let mut output = Vec::new();
    let mut terminal = Terminal::new(&mut output);
    
    terminal.restore_cursor().unwrap();
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Output bytes: {:?}", output);
    assert_eq!(output, b"\x1b8");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: restore_cursor writes correct sequence");
}

#[test]
fn test_save_restore_round_trip() {
    let mut output = Vec::new();
    let mut terminal = Terminal::new(&mut output);
    
    // Save, move, restore pattern
    terminal.save_cursor().unwrap();
    terminal.move_to(10, 5).unwrap();
    terminal.restore_cursor().unwrap();
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Full sequence: {:?}", String::from_utf8_lossy(&output));
    
    // Should contain save, move, restore in order
    let s = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output);
    assert!(s.contains("\x1b7"));
    assert!(s.contains("\x1b8"));
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: save/restore round trip works");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `save_cursor()` writes correct DEC sequence (\x1b7)
- [ ] `restore_cursor()` writes correct DEC sequence (\x1b8)
- [ ] Methods return io::Result for error handling
- [ ] All 3 unit tests pass with logging
- [ ] Existing Terminal tests still pass (no regression)","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Sequences are correct
- [ ] Terminal methods work
- [ ] Unit tests pass

-{"id":"bd-1qlr","title":"E2E: Headless Demo Showcase Testing","description":"# Headless Demo Showcase Testing

## Current State
- demo_showcase has --headless-smoke flag
- --headless-dump-json outputs frame stats
- --headless-size sets virtual terminal size
- Not systematically tested in CI

## Goal
Comprehensive automated testing of demo_showcase without requiring TTY.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Startup/Shutdown
- Clean startup in headless mode
- Clean shutdown with --exit-after-tour
- Graceful handling of missing TTY
- Frame limit respected (--max-frames)

### 2. Tour Mode
- All tour steps execute
- Each screen renders without panic
- Timing is deterministic (--seed)
- Exit after tour works

### 3. Frame Statistics
- JSON output is valid
- Frame count matches expectation
- Cell change counts reasonable
- No dropped frames in headless

### 4. Capability Presets
- --cap-preset minimal works
- --cap-preset no_hyperlinks works
- Graceful degradation verified

### 5. Size Variations
- 80x24 (standard)
- 132x43 (wide)
- 40x12 (small)
- 200x60 (large)

### 6. Determinism
- Same --seed produces same output
- Multiple runs are reproducible

## Implementation

\`\`\`bash
# Test script pattern
cargo run --bin demo_showcase -- \
  --headless-smoke \
  --tour \
  --exit-after-tour \
  --headless-size 80x24 \
  --seed 12345 \
  --max-frames 1000 \
  --headless-dump-json 2>&1 | jq .
\`\`\`

### Assertions
- Exit code 0
- JSON output valid
- Frame count in expected range
- No error messages in stderr

## Files to Create/Modify
- tests/e2e_demo_headless.rs - headless demo tests
- scripts/demo_showcase_ci.sh - CI test script

## Acceptance Criteria
- All headless modes tested
- Determinism verified
- Size variations covered
- CI integration complete","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:46:48.276570965Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:46:48.283647528Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qlr","depends_on_id":"bd-yps6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:46:48.283601191Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1rfq","title":"[EPIC] Comprehensive Unit Test Coverage (No Mocks)","description":"# Epic: Comprehensive Unit Test Coverage Without Mocks

## Goal
Achieve complete unit test coverage across all OpenTUI modules using REAL objects only - no mocks, fakes, or stubs.

## Current State
- 522 unit tests across 50 modules
- Critical gaps: renderer (1 test), ansi/sequences (0 tests), link.rs (2 tests)
- No mocking framework used (good - keep it that way)

## Success Criteria
- All public APIs have unit tests
- All edge cases documented and tested
- No mocking libraries introduced
- Coverage target: 90%+ for critical modules","notes":"Duplicate of bd-tv2h (EPIC: Unit Test Coverage Infrastructure). All unit test tasks are now tracked under the proper EPIC structure.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:43:30.929140589Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:59.799754928Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:51:59.799609877Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-1rov","title":"E2E: Golden File Rendering Verification","description":"# E2E: Golden File Rendering Verification (Consolidated)

Consolidated from bd-1rov and bd-2tld.

## Goal
Verify rendering output against known-good golden files for visual regression detection.

## Golden File Format
```
# Golden: tour_step_5_palette_open
# Size: 80x24
# Generated: 2026-01-28
# Capabilities: truecolor, hyperlinks
+--------------------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  [H] Help  [/] Palette  [T] Tour  [☀] Theme                    demo_showcase  |
...
```

## Test Scenarios

### Basic Rendering
- Empty buffer render
- Single character render
- Full screen text render
- Box drawing characters

### Color Rendering  
- TrueColor output verification
- 256-color fallback verification
- 16-color fallback verification
- Color attributes (bold, dim)

### Complex Rendering
- Alpha blending output
- Scissored content clipping
- Overlapping layers
- Wide characters (CJK, emoji)

### Demo Showcase Frames
- Each tour screen as golden file
- Help overlay capture
- Debug panel capture
- Command palette capture

## Comparison Logic
1. Exact match for deterministic output
2. Fuzzy match for timing-dependent content
3. Diff output on mismatch with line numbers
4. HTML diff visualization for debugging

## Update Workflow
- BLESS=1 env var to regenerate golden files
- Review process for golden file changes
- CI blocks on golden file mismatch
- Git diff shows golden changes in PRs

## Files to Create
- tests/golden/ - golden file directory
- tests/common/golden.rs - comparison utilities
- tests/golden/*.golden - actual golden files

## Acceptance Criteria
- 20+ golden file test cases
- CI fails on visual regression
- Easy update workflow with BLESS=1
- Diff visualization on failure
- Captures for all major UI states","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:45:51.469268214Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:22:33.834267943Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rov","depends_on_id":"bd-2tld","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:50:56.428446518Z","created_by":"claude"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rov","depends_on_id":"bd-yps6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:45:51.476295064Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1rxc","title":"Subtask: Renderer Hit Grid Tests","description":"# Renderer Hit Grid Tests

## Scope  
Test src/renderer/hitgrid.rs - currently only 4 tests.

## Test Cases

### Registration
1. test_register_single_area - one widget registered
2. test_register_multiple_areas - many widgets registered
3. test_register_overlapping - overlapping areas (later wins)
4. test_register_adjacent - touching but not overlapping

### Hit Testing
5. test_hit_inside - point inside registered area
6. test_hit_outside - point outside all areas
7. test_hit_boundary - point exactly on edge
8. test_hit_corner - point at corner
9. test_hit_between_areas - point in gap between areas

### Clear Operations
10. test_clear_removes_all - clear() removes all registrations
11. test_clear_then_register - can register after clear

### Edge Cases
12. test_zero_size_area - 0x0 widget registration
13. test_negative_coordinates - if supported
14. test_large_grid - 1000x1000 grid performance
15. test_many_widgets - 1000 widgets registered

### ID Management
16. test_widget_ids_unique - each registration gets unique ID
17. test_widget_id_returned - registration returns the ID

## Files to Modify
- src/renderer/hitgrid.rs - expand tests module

## Acceptance Criteria
- 17 new hit grid tests
- All boundary conditions covered
- Overlap behavior documented","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:47:52.087817497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:18.981828355Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:59:18.981760458Z","close_reason":"Extended hit grid tests from 25 to 37 tests. Added: adjacent regions (horizontal/vertical), widget ID preservation, max coordinates, diagonal layouts, row/column widget patterns, registration order verification, partial overlap chains, clone behavior, and 1x1/1xN/Nx1 grid edge cases. All 37 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rxc","depends_on_id":"bd-2e6f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:47:52.098848062Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

3. Archive coverage reports as artifacts
4. Post coverage diff on PRs

-{"id":"bd-1sbn","title":"Subtask: PTY Test Stabilization","description":"# PTY Test Stabilization

## Scope
Review and stabilize each of the 11 ignored PTY tests.

## Current Tests to Stabilize

1. test_tour_mode_terminal_lifecycle
   - Verify: alt screen, cursor hide, mouse enable
   - Issue: timing sensitivity
   
2. test_tour_mode_all_screens  
   - Verify: all tour screens render
   - Issue: screen transition timing
   
3. test_frame_rendering_correctness
   - Verify: ANSI sequences correct
   - Issue: output buffering

4-11. (Other PTY tests - need review)

## Stabilization Steps

### For Each Test:
1. Run 10x locally to identify flakiness
2. Add explicit waits where needed
3. Increase timeouts for slow CI
4. Add retry logic for transient failures
5. Add cleanup on failure

### Common Issues:
- Race conditions between send and receive
- Output buffering delays
- Signal handling interruptions
- PTY allocation failures

### Fixes:
- Use select() with timeout instead of sleep()
- Flush output explicitly
- Add sequence markers for synchronization
- Graceful degradation on allocation failure

## Files to Modify
- tests/pty_e2e.rs - stabilize tests
- tests/common/pty.rs - add helpers

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each test passes 100/100 runs locally
- No flakiness in CI (measure over 20 CI runs)
- Timeout increased appropriately

## Scope
Review and stabilize each of the 11 ignored PTY tests.

## Current Tests to Stabilize

1. test_tour_mode_terminal_lifecycle

   - Issue: timing sensitivity
   
2. test_tour_mode_all_screens

   - Issue: screen transition timing
   
3. test_frame_rendering_correctness
   - Verify: ANSI sequences correct
   - Issue: output buffering

4-11. (Other PTY tests - need review)

## Stabilization Steps

### For Each Test:
1. Run 10x locally to identify flakiness
2. Add explicit waits where needed
3. Increase timeouts for slow CI
4. Add retry logic for transient failures
5. Add cleanup on failure

### Common Issues:
- Race conditions between send and receive
- Output buffering delays
- Signal handling interruptions
- PTY allocation failures

### Fixes:
- Use select() with timeout instead of sleep()
- Flush output explicitly
- Add sequence markers for synchronization
- Graceful degradation on allocation failure

## Files to Modify
- tests/pty_e2e.rs - stabilize tests
- tests/common/pty.rs - add helpers

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each test passes 100/100 runs locally
- No flakiness in CI (measure over 20 CI runs)
- Timeout increased appropriately

 {"id":"bd-1t2","title":"demo_showcase UI: inspector/perf panel (RenderStats + capabilities)","description":"# demo_showcase UI — Inspector / Perf Panel (RenderStats + Capabilities)

## Purpose

A flagship demo should make performance and terminal capability behavior visible. This panel (or overlay) should show:

- `Renderer::stats()` (FPS, frame time, cells updated, buffer bytes)
- terminal capabilities (truecolor, sync output, hyperlinks, mouse, paste, focus)
- current demo mode flags (tour on/off, threaded on/off, fps cap)

This turns the demo into a credible engineering tool instead of a flashy animation.

## Minimum Data to Display

- FPS + frame time (ms)
- cells updated last frame
- estimated total memory (buffers + hitgrid)
- “sync output” active/inactive
- “true color” active/inactive
- hyperlink support yes/no
- mouse tracking yes/no
- bracketed paste yes/no

## Rendering Notes

- Must be legible at small sizes: show a compact mode (2–3 lines) when narrow.
- Use a clear visual hierarchy (labels dim, values bright, warnings colored).
- Optionally show a tiny live sparkline chart using `GrayscaleBuffer`.

## Integration

- Panel should update every frame and reflect real numbers.
- Clicking toggles (if implemented) should use HitGrid.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- The inspector/perf UI displays: FPS/frame time, cells updated last frame, memory estimate, and effective capability flags.
- The panel/overlay updates every frame and reflects real data from `Renderer::stats()` and `Renderer::capabilities()` (plus demo overrides).
- Missing capabilities and disabled features are surfaced as readable warnings (status/inspector).
- A compact rendering mode exists for narrow terminals.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:39.373078160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:40:41.895480508Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:40:41.895351748Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","features","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1t2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ob","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:07.313409545Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t2","depends_on_id":"bd-1pd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:06.747300687Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t2","depends_on_id":"bd-c75","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:24:39.402043390Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t2","depends_on_id":"bd-pnln","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:55:17.396173375Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1t2","depends_on_id":"bd-po1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:32:06.214292493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1t6h","title":"Unit tests: Edge cases for buffer operations","description":"# Unit Tests: Buffer Operation Edge Cases

## Scope
Add edge case tests for buffer operations that current tests don't cover.

## Test Areas

### Boundary Conditions
- [ ] Zero-size buffer (0x0)
- [ ] Single cell buffer (1x1)
- [ ] Very wide buffer (10000x1)
- [ ] Very tall buffer (1x10000)
- [ ] Maximum size buffer (stress test)

### Drawing Edge Cases
- [ ] Draw at exact boundary
- [ ] Draw extending past boundary
- [ ] Draw with negative coordinates (signed)
- [ ] Draw with very large coordinates

### Scissor Edge Cases
- [ ] Scissor larger than buffer
- [ ] Scissor with negative coords
- [ ] Deeply nested scissors (100+ levels)
- [ ] Scissor that results in empty area

### Opacity Edge Cases
- [ ] Opacity > 1.0
- [ ] Opacity < 0.0
- [ ] Very small opacity (1e-10)
- [ ] Deeply nested opacity (100+ levels)

### Wide Character Edge Cases
- [ ] Wide char at right edge
- [ ] Wide char overlapping scissor
- [ ] Continuation cell at buffer start
- [ ] Overwriting half of wide char

## Expected Behavior
- No panics
- Graceful handling (clamp, skip, or error)
- Consistent state after edge case","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T19:17:31.478897368Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T19:17:31.501042643Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["buffer","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1t6h","depends_on_id":"bd-pw23","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T19:17:31.501010223Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1tl","title":"EditorView Visual Navigation","description":"## Overview
Implement visual cursor navigation that respects text wrapping.

## Scope
```rust
impl EditorView {
    /// Move cursor up in the visual (wrapped) view.
    pub fn move_up_visual(&mut self);

    /// Move cursor down in the visual (wrapped) view.
    pub fn move_down_visual(&mut self);

    /// Get the start of the current visual line.
    pub fn visual_line_start(&self) -> usize;

    /// Get the end of the current visual line.
    pub fn visual_line_end(&self) -> usize;

    /// Move to visual line start.
    pub fn move_to_visual_line_start(&mut self);

    /// Move to visual line end.
    pub fn move_to_visual_line_end(&mut self);
}
```

## Implementation Details
Visual navigation requires:
1. Getting current cursor position in visual coordinates
2. Using line cache to map between logical and visual lines
3. Moving within visual lines respecting column position

## Files to Modify
- src/text/editor.rs - add visual navigation methods

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (minimum 12 tests with detailed logging)
- test_visual_move_up_no_wrap
- test_visual_move_up_with_wrap
- test_visual_move_up_within_wrapped_line
- test_visual_move_down_no_wrap
- test_visual_move_down_with_wrap
- test_visual_move_down_within_wrapped_line
- test_visual_line_start
- test_visual_line_end
- test_visual_nav_preserves_column
- test_visual_nav_at_buffer_start
- test_visual_nav_at_buffer_end
- test_visual_nav_wide_characters

### Detailed Test Logging Framework
Each test MUST log visual and logical coordinates:

```rust
#[test]
fn test_visual_move_up_with_wrap() {
    // Create text that wraps at width 10
    let text = "Short\nThis is a very long line that will wrap multiple times\nEnd";
    let buffer = EditBuffer::with_text(text);
    let mut view = EditorView::new(buffer);
    view.set_wrap_mode(WrapMode::Word);
    view.set_viewport(0, 0, 10, 10);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text: {:?}", text);
    eprintln!("[TEST] Viewport width: 10");
    eprintln!("[TEST] WrapMode: Word");
    
    // Move to middle of the long line (which is wrapped)
    view.edit_buffer_mut().move_to(1, 25);  // Logical line 1, col 25
    
    let cursor = view.edit_buffer().cursor();
    let visual = view.visual_cursor(10, 10);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Initial position:");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   Logical: row={} col={} offset={}", 
        cursor.row, cursor.col, cursor.offset);
    eprintln!("[TEST]   Visual: row={} col={}", 
        visual.visual_row, visual.visual_col);
    
    // Move up in visual view
    view.move_up_visual();
    
    let cursor = view.edit_buffer().cursor();
    let visual = view.visual_cursor(10, 10);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] After move_up_visual:");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   Logical: row={} col={} offset={}", 
        cursor.row, cursor.col, cursor.offset);
    eprintln!("[TEST]   Visual: row={} col={}", 
        visual.visual_row, visual.visual_col);
    
    // Visual row should decrease by 1, but logical row might stay same
    // (if we're still within the wrapped line)
    assert!(visual.visual_row < 3, "Visual row should decrease");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Visual up navigation works with wrapping");
}

#[test]
fn test_visual_nav_preserves_column() {
    let text = "Short\nMedium line\nAnother short";
    let buffer = EditBuffer::with_text(text);
    let mut view = EditorView::new(buffer);
    view.set_wrap_mode(WrapMode::None);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text:");
    for (i, line) in text.lines().enumerate() {
        eprintln!("[TEST]   Line {}: {:?} (len={})", i, line, line.len());
    }
    
    // Position at column 8 in middle line
    view.edit_buffer_mut().move_to(1, 8);
    
    let cursor = view.edit_buffer().cursor();
    eprintln!("[TEST] Initial: row={} col={}", cursor.row, cursor.col);
    
    // Move up - "Short" only has 5 chars, so col should clamp
    view.move_up_visual();
    
    let cursor = view.edit_buffer().cursor();
    eprintln!("[TEST] After up: row={} col={}", cursor.row, cursor.col);
    assert_eq!(cursor.row, 0);
    assert!(cursor.col <= 5, "Column should clamp to line length");
    
    // Move down twice - back to middle, then to "Another short"
    view.move_down_visual();
    view.move_down_visual();
    
    let cursor = view.edit_buffer().cursor();
    eprintln!("[TEST] After 2x down: row={} col={}", cursor.row, cursor.col);
    assert_eq!(cursor.row, 2);
    // Column should attempt to restore to 8 if line is long enough
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Column position preserved/clamped correctly");
}

#[test]
fn test_visual_line_start_and_end() {
    let text = "This is a line that will definitely wrap when viewport is narrow";
    let buffer = EditBuffer::with_text(text);
    let mut view = EditorView::new(buffer);
    view.set_wrap_mode(WrapMode::Word);
    view.set_viewport(0, 0, 15, 10);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text: {:?}", text);
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text length: {} bytes", text.len());
    eprintln!("[TEST] Viewport width: 15");
    
    // Move to middle of text
    view.edit_buffer_mut().move_to_offset(30);
    
    let cursor = view.edit_buffer().cursor();
    let visual = view.visual_cursor(15, 10);
    eprintln!("[TEST] Position: offset={} visual_row={} visual_col={}",
        cursor.offset, visual.visual_row, visual.visual_col);
    
    let start = view.visual_line_start();
    let end = view.visual_line_end();
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Visual line boundaries:");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   start offset: {}", start);
    eprintln!("[TEST]   end offset: {}", end);
    eprintln!("[TEST]   visual line text: {:?}", &text[start..end]);
    
    assert!(start <= cursor.offset);
    assert!(end >= cursor.offset);
    assert!(end - start <= 15, "Visual line should fit in viewport");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Visual line start/end computed correctly");
}

#[test]
fn test_visual_nav_wide_characters() {
    // CJK characters are 2 columns wide
    let text = "ABC\u{4e2d}\u{6587}\u{6d4b}\u{8bd5}DEF";  // "ABC中文测试DEF"
    let buffer = EditBuffer::with_text(text);
    let mut view = EditorView::new(buffer);
    view.set_wrap_mode(WrapMode::Char);
    view.set_viewport(0, 0, 8, 5);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text: {:?}", text);
    eprintln!("[TEST] Expected widths:");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   'ABC' = 3 cols");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   '中文测试' = 8 cols (4 chars x 2)");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   'DEF' = 3 cols");
    eprintln!("[TEST]   Total = 14 cols, viewport = 8");
    
    // Should wrap somewhere
    view.edit_buffer_mut().move_to_offset(0);
    
    let visual = view.visual_cursor(8, 5);
    eprintln!("[TEST] At offset 0: visual_row={}", visual.visual_row);
    
    // Move to end
    view.edit_buffer_mut().move_to_offset(text.len());
    let visual = view.visual_cursor(8, 5);
    eprintln!("[TEST] At end: visual_row={}", visual.visual_row);
    
    // Navigate up/down and verify no crash on wide chars
    view.move_up_visual();
    let cursor = view.edit_buffer().cursor();
    eprintln!("[TEST] After up: offset={}", cursor.offset);
    
    // Should not be in middle of a CJK character
    let text_bytes = text.as_bytes();
    assert!(text.is_char_boundary(cursor.offset), 
        "Cursor should be at valid char boundary");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Wide character navigation works");
}

#[test]
fn test_visual_nav_at_buffer_boundaries() {
    let text = "Line 1\nLine 2";
    let buffer = EditBuffer::with_text(text);
    let mut view = EditorView::new(buffer);
    view.set_wrap_mode(WrapMode::None);
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] Text: {:?}", text);
    
    // At start, try to move up
    view.edit_buffer_mut().move_to(0, 0);
    eprintln!("[TEST] At buffer start, calling move_up_visual");
    view.move_up_visual();
    
    let cursor = view.edit_buffer().cursor();
    eprintln!("[TEST] After up at start: row={} col={}", cursor.row, cursor.col);
    assert_eq!(cursor.row, 0, "Should stay at start");
    
    // At end, try to move down
    view.edit_buffer_mut().move_to(1, 6);
    eprintln!("[TEST] At buffer end, calling move_down_visual");
    view.move_down_visual();
    
    let cursor = view.edit_buffer().cursor();
    eprintln!("[TEST] After down at end: row={} col={}", cursor.row, cursor.col);
    assert_eq!(cursor.row, 1, "Should stay at end");
    
    eprintln!("[TEST] PASS: Boundary conditions handled (no crash)");
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] move_up_visual/move_down_visual navigate visual lines correctly
- [ ] Navigation works within wrapped logical lines
- [ ] Column position preserved when possible
- [ ] Column clamped when target line is shorter
- [ ] visual_line_start/end return correct byte offsets
- [ ] Wide characters (CJK) handled correctly (no mid-char positioning)
- [ ] Boundary conditions handled gracefully (no crash)
- [ ] Works correctly with all wrap modes (None/Char/Word)
- [ ] All 12+ unit tests pass with detailed logging
- [ ] Each test logs both logical and visual coordinates
- [ ] Tests log text content at cursor positions

Dependencies:
  -> bd-2sk (blocks) - TextBufferView Line Info Cache
  -> bd-1ms (blocks) - EditBuffer Word Boundary Navigation

Dependents:
  <- bd-rqd (blocks) - EditorView Scroll Margins and Selection","acceptance_criteria":"- [ ] Up/down navigate visual lines correctly
- [ ] Column position preserved when possible
- [ ] Works correctly with wrapped text
- [ ] All 8+ unit tests pass

 {"id":"bd-3k9i","title":"EPIC: demo_showcase Unit Test Expansion","description":"# EPIC: demo_showcase Unit Test Expansion

Parent EPIC for unit tests specific to the demo_showcase binary.

## Background

Recent bug fixes in demo_showcase introduced new functionality that needs unit testing:
1. Palette scroll offset calculation (fix for off-screen selection)
2. Bounded log VecDeque with dropped counter
3. Metrics computation with frame wraparound
4. extract_buffer_row overflow prevention
5. Headless check mode functions

## Goals

1. Unit test all recent bug fixes
2. Ensure edge cases are covered
3. Prevent regression of fixed bugs
4. Document expected behavior in tests

## Child Tasks

### Core Bug Fix Tests
- bd-v4ul: Palette Scroll Offset tests
  - Scroll offset calculation when selected > visible
  - is_selected uses actual index, not display position
  - Edge cases: empty filter, single item

- bd-1cp8: Bounded Log VecDeque tests
  - MAX_LOGS constant enforced
  - Oldest entries evicted on overflow
  - Dropped counter increments atomically
  - drain_log_queue creates notification

- bd-2inw: Metrics Compute Wraparound tests
  - FRAME_CYCLE prevents u64 overflow
  - FPS variation bounded by sin()
  - Float clamped before u32 cast
  - Division by zero prevented

- bd-3mtq: extract_buffer_row Overflow Prevention tests
  - saturating_add prevents overflow
  - Empty result on overflow
  - Boundary conditions at buffer edge

### Headless Mode Tests
- bd-35ey: Headless Check Functions tests
  - run_check_layout mode transitions
  - run_check_config parsing validation
  - run_check_palette filter/selection
  - run_check_hitgrid ID ranges
  - run_check_logs ring buffer behavior

## Dependencies

All child tasks depend on:
- bd-2azg: Test Fixture Library (assertion helpers)

## Test Approach

1. **No mocks**: Test real App, PaletteState, etc.
2. **Edge cases first**: Test boundaries, overflows, empty cases
3. **Regression prevention**: Each test documents the bug it prevents
4. **Deterministic**: Use fixed seeds where randomness involved

## Success Criteria

- [ ] All 5 bug fix areas have unit tests
- [ ] Edge cases documented and tested
- [ ] Tests run without demo_showcase binary (pure unit tests)
- [ ] Coverage ≥90% for touched functions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:44:00.069604996Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:29:21.983052131Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3k9i","depends_on_id":"bd-26vw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:44:00.082611633Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3l0","title":"demo_showcase spec: keybindings + interaction model (keyboard/mouse/focus)","description":"# demo_showcase Spec — Keybindings + Interaction Model

## Philosophy

- **Discoverable**: status bar + help overlay always tell you what to do.
- **Low cognitive load**: a small set of global chords; panel-local keys only when focused.
- **Mouse is first-class**: click to focus; scroll to scroll; clickable buttons prove HitGrid.

## Global Keybindings (Always Active)

- `Ctrl+Q` — Quit
- `F1` — Help overlay (toggle)
- `Ctrl+P` — Command palette (toggle)
- `Ctrl+T` — Tour mode (toggle / start from step 1)
- `Ctrl+D` — Toggle OpenTUI debug overlay (fps/cells/memory)
- `Tab` / `Shift+Tab` — Cycle focused panel
- `Ctrl+R` — Force full redraw (`renderer.invalidate()`)

## Panel Focus Model

There is always exactly one **focused panel**:

- Focus is drawn (border highlight + subtle glow)
- Keyboard events route primarily to the focused panel
- Mouse click inside a panel sets focus

Panels:
- Sidebar
- Editor
- Preview
- Logs

## Mouse Interaction Model (Must Exercise HitGrid)

- Click on sidebar items selects section
- Click on top bar “buttons” toggles overlays (Help / Palette / Tour)
- Scroll wheel over sidebar/logs scrolls that panel
- Click on hyperlink-like rows shows visual pressed feedback (even though link open is handled by terminal)

Implementation hint: register hit areas for:

- Each sidebar row
- Each top-bar button
- Selected preview “tabs”

## Input Events to Explicitly Surface

The app should *visibly react* to:

- Focus gained/lost events:
  - When focus lost: pause animations, show “PAUSED (focus lost)” badge
  - When focus gained: resume
- Bracketed paste:
  - Paste into editor inserts text and shows toast “Pasted N chars”
- Resize:
  - Layout recompute + toast “Resized to WxH”

## Editor Keybindings (When Editor Focused)

Keep it close to existing `examples/editor.rs`:

- Arrow keys: move cursor
- `Ctrl+Left/Right`: word navigation
- `Home/End`: line start/end
- `PageUp/PageDown`: scroll
- `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y`: undo/redo
- `Ctrl+W`: cycle wrap mode (None → Word → Char)
- `Ctrl+L`: toggle line numbers

## Overlay Keybindings

- Help overlay:
  - `Esc` / `F1` to close
- Command palette:
  - `Esc` close
  - `Enter` activate selected action
  - Up/Down navigate list
- Tour:
  - `Enter` / `Space` next step
  - `Backspace` previous step
  - `Esc` exit tour","acceptance_criteria":"Checklist:
- [ ] Global keybindings defined and minimal
- [ ] Focus model defined and consistent
- [ ] Mouse behaviors defined (click, scroll, hit testing targets)
- [ ] Focus gained/lost behavior defined (pause/resume)
- [ ] Bracketed paste behavior defined (insert + toast)
- [ ] Editor keys defined (undo/redo, wrap, line numbers, navigation)
- [ ] Overlay keys defined (help/palette/tour)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:05:42.145358418Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:49:13.775553150Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:49:13.775424420Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","design","input"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3l0","depends_on_id":"bd-2jc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:05:42.168791836Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3l0","depends_on_id":"bd-2mu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:55.958980961Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-3le2","title":"E2E: Enable and Stabilize PTY Tests in CI","description":"# Enable PTY E2E Tests in CI - CRITICAL

## Current State
- 11 PTY tests exist in tests/pty_e2e.rs
- ALL marked #[ignore] - never run
- PTY infrastructure exists (tests/common/pty.rs)
- Tests verify actual terminal lifecycle

## PTY Tests to Enable

### Terminal Lifecycle Tests
- test_tour_mode_terminal_lifecycle - alt screen, cursor, mouse
- test_tour_mode_all_screens - multi-screen navigation
- test_frame_rendering_correctness - actual ANSI output

### Sequence Detection Tests
- ALT_SCREEN_ENTER/LEAVE detection
- CURSOR_HIDE/SHOW detection
- MOUSE_BUTTON_ENABLE/DISABLE detection
- MOUSE_MOTION_ENABLE detection

## Work Required

### 1. CI Infrastructure
- Add PTY test job to GitHub Actions
- Use appropriate runner (Linux with PTY support)
- Set reasonable timeout (PTY tests are slow)

### 2. Test Stabilization
- Review each ignored test
- Fix timing-sensitive assertions
- Add proper cleanup on failure
- Handle signal interruption

### 3. Artifact Capture
- Capture PTY output on failure
- Save to CI artifacts
- Include timestamp and test name

### 4. Documentation
- Document how to run PTY tests locally
- Document CI requirements
- Document common failure modes

## Files to Modify
- tests/pty_e2e.rs - remove #[ignore] after stabilizing
- .github/workflows/*.yml - add PTY test job
- tests/common/pty.rs - enhance stability

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 11 PTY tests pass reliably in CI

+{"id":"bd-3le2","title":"E2E: Enable and Stabilize PTY Tests in CI","description":"# Enable PTY E2E Tests in CI - CRITICAL

- 11 PTY tests exist in tests/pty_e2e.rs

- MOUSE_MOTION_ENABLE detection

## Work Required

### 1. CI Infrastructure
- Add PTY test job to GitHub Actions
- Use appropriate runner (Linux with PTY support)
- Set reasonable timeout (PTY tests are slow)

### 2. Test Stabilization
- Review each ignored test
- Fix timing-sensitive assertions
- Add proper cleanup on failure
- Handle signal interruption

### 3. Artifact Capture
- Capture PTY output on failure
- Save to CI artifacts
- Include timestamp and test name

### 4. Documentation
- Document how to run PTY tests locally
- Document CI requirements
- Document common failure modes

## Files to Modify
- tests/pty_e2e.rs - remove #[ignore] after stabilizing
- .github/workflows/*.yml - add PTY test job
- tests/common/pty.rs - enhance stability

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 11 PTY tests pass reliably in CI
- Flaky test rate < 1%
- Artifacts captured on failure

 {"id":"bd-3lio","title":"demo_showcase runtime: hard frame budget (--max-frames)","description":"# demo_showcase Runtime — Hard Frame Budget (`--max-frames`)

## Purpose

We need a deterministic, mode-independent way to bound runtime so we can:

- run PTY E2E tests without hanging
- run quick headless smoke checks with consistent cost
- record short, repeatable demo clips

This bead implements the *runtime semantics* of a hard frame budget.

## Behavior

- Flag: `--max-frames <N>`
- After the app has **presented N frames**, it exits 0.

Counting rules:

- Count a “frame” after a successful `present()` (or after a successful headless render+diff step).
- The counter applies in **all modes**:
  - interactive normal
  - interactive tour
  - headless smoke
  - headless tour

Exit contract:

- Print a stable summary line to stdout:
  - `EXIT_OK reason=max_frames frames=<N> last_dirty_cells=<K>`

## Design Notes

- This is intentionally separate from `--exit-after-tour`:
  - `--exit-after-tour` is a tour completion feature.
  - `--max-frames` is a generic safety bound and developer tool.

## Downstream Consumers

- PTY E2E harness (`bd-61g0`) uses `--max-frames` as a hard safety bound.
- Headless smoke/snapshots (`bd-2el`, `bd-1n67`, `bd-2649`, `bd-bqd1`, `bd-2bnv`) use `--max-frames` to keep runs fast and deterministic.","acceptance_criteria":"Done when:
- `--max-frames <N>` exits 0 after presenting N frames in interactive mode.
- `--max-frames <N>` also bounds headless runs consistently (smoke and tour), counting frames deterministically.
- A stable exit summary line is printed (`EXIT_OK reason=max_frames ...`) and includes `last_dirty_cells`.
- PTY E2E tests can use `--max-frames` as a hang-proof safety bound.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.243660372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T02:27:36.270704114Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T02:27:36.270561729Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","runtime","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lio","depends_on_id":"bd-29z","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.259983646Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lio","depends_on_id":"bd-2iv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.267009792Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lio","depends_on_id":"bd-z1b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:16.264016443Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3nbv","title":"E2E tests: Input handling and event flow","description":"# E2E Test: Input Handling & Event Flow

## Scope
Test complete input handling from raw bytes to parsed events to application response.

## Test Scenarios

### Keyboard Input Flow
- [ ] Single keypress → KeyEvent
- [ ] Modifier combinations (Ctrl+X, Alt+Y, etc.)
- [ ] Function keys (F1-F12)
- [ ] Special keys (arrows, home, end, etc.)
- [ ] Unicode character input

### Mouse Input Flow
- [ ] Click → MouseEvent with position
- [ ] Drag sequence (press → move → release)
- [ ] Scroll wheel events
- [ ] Mouse position accuracy

### Paste Input
- [ ] Bracketed paste detection
- [ ] Large paste handling
- [ ] Binary data in paste

### Event to Render Response
- [ ] Keypress → buffer change → present
- [ ] Mouse click → hit test → response
- [ ] Resize event → buffer resize → present

### Logging Points
```jsonl
{"step": "input_raw", "bytes": [27, 91, ...]}
{"step": "parse", "event": {"type": "key", "code": "A", "modifiers": []}}
{"step": "handle", "action": "insert_char", "char": "A"}
{"step": "render", "changed_cells": 1}
```

## Edge Cases
- [ ] Partial escape sequences (interrupted input)
- [ ] Rapid input flood
- [ ] Invalid/malformed sequences
- [ ] Timeout handling","notes":"Merged into bd-1nfp (E2E: Input Simulation for Complex Workflows). The input flow testing scenarios and logging points from this bead have been incorporated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T19:16:59.535467058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:04.569331009Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:25:04.569188423Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","input","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3nbv","depends_on_id":"bd-2vka","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T19:16:59.557573540Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
